description: To create fabric or structure by interlacing threads, strands, or elements—passing over and under, building coherent whole from separate parts. 'Selomi' names both the physical craft and the metaphorical act of weaving together ideas, stories, or relationships into unified fabric.
sound_symbolism: The word enacts weaving's rhythm. 'Se' begins with the sibilant of strands sliding past each other. Then 'lo' loops with liquid over-under motion. Finally 'mi' meshes with nasal joining, the interlaced whole emerging.
grammatical_notes: As verb: 'mi themo selomi' (I weave something). As noun: 'wela selomi' (good weaving / fine woven work). Extends metaphorically: 'mi talu selomi' (I weave a story).

pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Weaving is among humanity's oldest technologies—creating warmth, shelter, and beauty from simple strands through patient interlacing.
pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Reality is woven of interdependent threads—nothing exists alone, everything interlaced with everything else.
